title: HSV color picker with saturation/value area, hue slider, opacity slider, and hex input
purpose: Provides a color selection interface that allows users to pick colors using HSV color space with additional alpha and hex input controls.
module_context: Exports a React functional component named ColorPicker that manages color state and handles user interactions for color selection.

methods.name: rgbToHsv
methods.normalized_params: (r: number, g: number, b: number): { h: number; s: number; v: number }

steps.content: Determine the maximum and minimum RGB values.
Calculate the chroma and saturation based on the maximum value.
Set the value to the maximum RGB value.
Compute the hue based on which RGB component is the maximum.
Normalize the hue to a 0-360 degree scale.

methods.name: hsvToRgb
methods.normalized_params: (h: number, s: number, v: number): { r: number; g: number; b: number }

steps.content: Convert saturation and value from percentages to decimals.
Calculate the chroma and intermediate x value.
Determine the primary RGB components based on the hue sector.
Add the lightness adjustment to each RGB component.
Initialize state for open status, hue, saturation, value, and alpha.
Set up a reference to the saturation/value area element.
Set up a reference to the popover element.
Synchronize HSV values from the incoming color when the picker is closed.
Add event listener for clicks outside the picker to close it.
Define a callback to emit color changes based on HSV and alpha values.
Handle pointer down events on the saturation/value area to update saturation and value.
Handle pointer move events on the saturation/value area to update saturation and value.

methods.name: emitColor
methods.normalized_params: (h: number, s: number, v: number, a: number): void

steps.content: Convert the HSV values to RGB components.
Call the onChange handler with the new RGB color and alpha value.
Capture the pointer on the saturation/value area element.
Calculate the saturation and value from the pointer position.
Update the saturation and value state.
Emit the new color based on updated HSV and alpha values.
Check if the pointer is captured on the saturation/value area.
Calculate the saturation and value from the pointer position.
Update the saturation and value state.
Emit the new color based on updated HSV and alpha values.
Extract the hex input value and remove any leading hash.
Validate that the input is a valid 6-character hexadecimal string.
Parse the hex string into a color object.
Update the color state with the parsed color and current alpha value.
